whatItMeasures: TIBC measures the maximum amount of iron the blood can carry by quantifying the total capacity of transferrin — the protein that transports iron — to bind iron; it is used alongside serum iron and ferritin to diagnose iron deficiency or iron overload.
causesOfHigh: Iron deficiency anemia (the body produces more transferrin when iron is scarce)
causesOfLow: Iron overload or hemochromatosis (transferrin is saturated)
Chronic inflammatory diseases (anemia of chronic disease)

high: Symptoms of the underlying iron deficiency: fatigue, pale skin, shortness of breath
low: Symptoms of iron overload: joint pain, fatigue, abdominal pain
Symptoms of liver disease: jaundice, abdominal swelling
Symptoms of chronic inflammation: fatigue, pain, swelling

citations.title: Iron-Deficiency Anemia
citations.source: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI)
